perturbation.original_text: Developer shall return to FDI or destroy all copies of the Sample Data in its possession or control.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer shall, at FDI's request, return to FDI all copies of the Sample Data in its possession or control. Developer is under no obligation to destroy the data.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the obligation to destroy the data to optional, at FDI's request, and removes the requirement to destroy the data contradicts the original intent of the data being returned or destroyed, now it's just at FDI's request to return, and no longer needing to destroy the data. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 2 previously required destruction of data copies if not returned, ensuring no unauthorized copies remained. It is also contradicting Section 3, which states that 'Due to the valuable and proprietary nature of the Sample Data, the obligations assumed by Developer hereunder other than those stated in Section 1(c) shall be unlimited in time or territory.'. Now it is only returned at FDI's request, making the Section 3 contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Term .Due to the valuable and proprietary nature of the Sample Data, the obligations assumed by Developer hereunder other than those stated in Section 1(c) shall be unlimited in time or territory. 24. Ownership .Developer acknowledges that FDI owns the Sample Data and agrees not to challenge FDI’s ownership of the Data or the Sample Data. 5. Injunctive Relief. Developer agrees that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for unauthorized use or disclosure by it of any Sample Data, and that, in addition to all other remedies, Developer shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for Developer’s unauthorized use or disclosure, without the requirement of posting any bond. In the event that FDI deems it appropriate to initiate any action to enforce the obligations of Developer under this Agreement, and should Developer be held to have breached this Agreement, Developer shall reimburse FDI for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees. 6. Governing Law .This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York without regard to its conflicts of laws provisions. Frames Data Inc. By: _________________________ Name: Thomas Lamond Title: President [Developer] By: _________________________ Name: _______________________ Title: ________________________
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the developer's obligation regarding the Sample Data. The original text mandated either return or destruction of the data. The changed text makes destruction optional at FDI's request, contradicting Section 3, which stipulates that obligations regarding the data (other than those in Section 1(c)) are unlimited in time. The change makes the developer's responsibility conditional, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction, as the unlimited nature of the obligation is undermined. The change goes against the 'valuable and proprietary nature of the Sample Data' that is in Contradicted Text's section 3.

perturbation.original_text: Due to the valuable and proprietary nature of the Sample Data, the obligations assumed by Developer hereunder other than those stated in Section 1(c) shall be unlimited in time or territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Due to the potentially valuable nature of the Sample Data, the obligations assumed by Developer hereunder other than those stated in Section 1(c) shall be limited to the Assessment Period and the geographical location of Developer's principal place of business.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the 'unlimited' term and territory to being limited to the 'Assessment Period' and 'Developer's location' creates an in-text contradiction. This directly contradicts Section 5 (Injunctive Relief) which previously could be enforced without territorial or time limits. Now it could be argued Section 5 can only be enforced within the Assessment Period or within the Developer's geographical location.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Injunctive Relief. Developer agrees that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for unauthorized use or disclosure by it of any Sample Data, and that, in addition to all other remedies, Developer shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for Developer’s unauthorized use or disclosure, without the requirement of posting any bond. In the event that FDI deems it appropriate to initiate any action to enforce the obligations of Developer under this Agreement, and should Developer be held to have breached this Agreement, Developer shall reimburse FDI for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the developer's obligations are 'unlimited in time or territory'. The changed text limits these obligations to the 'Assessment Period' and the 'Developer's location'. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 5 (Injunctive Relief), which, based on the original text, could be enforced without territorial or time limits. Now, due to the change, Section 5 could be argued to only be enforceable within the Assessment Period or within the Developer's geographical location, directly conflicting with its original unlimited scope.

perturbation.original_text: Developer agrees that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for unauthorized use or disclosure by it of any Sample Data, and that, in addition to all other remedies, Developer shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for Developer’s unauthorized use or disclosure, without the requirement of posting any bond.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer agrees that money damages may not always be an adequate remedy for unauthorized use or disclosure by it of any Sample Data, and that, in addition to all other remedies, Developer may be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for Developer’s unauthorized use or disclosure, with the requirement of posting a bond at the court's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'would not be' to 'may not always be', and changing 'shall be entitled' to 'may be entitled', and adding the bond requirement, weakens FDI's ability to seek injunctive relief. This creates an in-text contradiction as Section 5 was originally written to strongly favor FDI, but the modification now allows for judicial discretion. Now it is less certain when injunctive relief is appropriate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Non-Disclosure Agreement is entered into by and between Frames Data Inc., a California corporation with a business address at 100 Avenue of the Americas, New York, New York 10013 (“FDI ”), and ________________________________, a ______________ __________________ with a business address at __________________ (“Developer ”). WHEREAS, FDI has compiled and maintains certain databases pertaining to ophthalmic frame style specifications which it updates and modifies from time to time that includes textual and graphic data relating to frames manufactured by various manufacturers (the “Data ”); Developer develops optical practice management software applications (the “Applications ”) and wishes to obtain a sample of certain Data (the “Sample Data ”) for the sole purpose of assessing whether Developer can adapt the Applications to be compatible with the Sample Data (the “Permitted Purpose ”); and FDI is willing to provide Developer with a sample of Sample Data for the Permitted Purpose for a limited time (the “Assessment Period ”) on the terms and conditions set forth below; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual promises made below, the parties agree as follows: 1. Non-Disclosure and Non-Use of Sample Data a. Non-Use .Developer shall not use any Sample Data for any purpose other than the Permitted Purpose. b. Non-Disclosure .Except as provided in subsection (c) below, Developer shall not disclose any Sample Data to any third party or to its own employees, agents or professional advisors except employees, agents or professional advisors who are required to have the Sample Data to implement the Permitted Purpose. Developer shall have each employee to whom any Sample Data will be disclosed, or who will have access to Sample Data, sign a nondisclosure agreement that is substantially similar to this Agreement. Developer shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of the Sample Data, including, without limitation, using the highest degree of care that Developer uses to protect its own confidential information. Developer shall notify FDI in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of Sample Data immediately after it becomes aware of such misuse or misappropriation. Developer shall not reproduce any Sample Data in any form or store it in a retrieval system or database without FDI’s prior written consent, except as Developer reasonably may require for the Permitted Purpose. c. Legal Compulsion .If Developer becomes legally compelled to disclose any Sample Data, Developer shall provide FDI with prompt prior written notice of such requirement so that FDI may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, or both, or waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement. 2. Return of Sample Data. When the Assessment Period is over, if the parties have not agreed to go forward and sign a comprehensive agreement that permits Developer to use the Data to modify its Applications and pursuant to which FDI will provide Developer with the technical documents that are necessary for Developer to do so, Developer shall return to FDI or destroy all copies of the Sample Data in its possession or control. 3. Term .Due to the valuable and proprietary nature of the Sample Data, the obligations assumed by Developer hereunder other than those stated in Section 1(c) shall be unlimited in time or territory. 24. Ownership .Developer acknowledges that FDI owns the Sample Data and agrees not to challenge FDI’s ownership of the Data or the Sample Data.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'money damages would not be an adequate remedy' and 'Developer shall be entitled to specific performance'. The changed text weakens this by saying 'money damages may not always be an adequate remedy' and 'Developer may be entitled to seek specific performance', and introduces a bond requirement. This weakening directly contradicts the intention of the NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT, which aims to strongly protect FDI's data by imposing strict obligations on the Developer. By making the remedies less certain and introducing judicial discretion, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction that undermines the agreement's purpose.

perturbation.original_text: Developer shall reimburse FDI for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer shall reimburse FDI for reasonable costs and expenses, at the court's discretion, including reasonable attorneys’ fees only if Developer's breach was intentional and malicious.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'at the court's discretion' and limiting attorneys' fees to 'intentional and malicious' breaches, this weakens FDI's position in recovering legal expenses. This contrasts the original agreement, which gave FDI more protection for their expenses. This creates in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Term .Due to the valuable and proprietary nature of the Sample Data, the obligations assumed by Developer hereunder other than those stated in Section 1(c) shall be unlimited in time or territory. 24. Ownership .Developer acknowledges that FDI owns the Sample Data and agrees not to challenge FDI’s ownership of the Data or the Sample Data. 5. Injunctive Relief. Developer agrees that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for unauthorized use or disclosure by it of any Sample Data, and that, in addition to all other remedies, Developer shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for Developer’s unauthorized use or disclosure, without the requirement of posting any bond. In the event that FDI deems it appropriate to initiate any action to enforce the obligations of Developer under this Agreement, and should Developer be held to have breached this Agreement, Developer shall reimburse FDI for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees. 6. Governing Law .This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York without regard to its conflicts of laws provisions. Frames Data Inc. By: _________________________ Name: Thomas Lamond Title: President [Developer] By: _________________________ Name: _______________________ Title: ________________________
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Developer shall reimburse FDI for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees'. The changed text modifies this to 'Developer shall reimburse FDI for reasonable costs and expenses, at the court's discretion, including reasonable attorneys’ fees only if Developer's breach was intentional and malicious.' The contradicted text, states 'Developer shall reimburse FDI for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees'. The modified text now imposes a condition (intentional and malicious breach) for the reimbursement of attorney's fees, and gives discretion to the court regarding 'reasonable costs and expenses' where there was none previously, directly contradicting the unconditional reimbursement clause in the contradicted text. This inconsistency regarding reimbursement of costs and attorneys' fees creates a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
